success_surface: Cell-level formula application with array logic, preservation of workbook integrity, and systematic verification
effective_behaviors: Used openpyxl library for formula insertion with style preservation
Created backup copy before any modifications to protect source data
Programmatically verified sheet names before worksheet access
Defined column and row range variables for clarity and maintainability
Constructed complex nested formulas as raw strings with proper absolute/relative reference syntax
Applied formulas iteratively to each cell in target range
Implemented try/except error handling during cell-by-cell operations
Performed sample verification by checking multiple cells after modification
Logged progress, affected count, and errors for execution transparency
evidence: shutil.copy(input_path, output_path) created backup before load_workbook()
formula string used $C$2:$C$41=$C2 pattern for proper reference scoping
try/except block wrapped each row's formula assignment
rows_modified counter tracked successfully processed cells
wb.save() executed only after all formula applications completed

memory_items.title: Workbook Integrity First
memory_items.lesson: Always create a backup copy of the source file before loading it for modifications.
memory_items.generalization: Preserve original file state using copy-before-write pattern; never modify source directly unless explicitly permitted.

memory_items.title: Formula Construction with Reference Scoping
memory_items.lesson: When building comparison formulas across groups, use mixed references ($column$start:end vs $column$current_row) to enable proper grouping logic.
memory_items.generalization: For conditional aggregation across ranges, construct formulas with absolute range anchors combined with relative current-row references to maintain context-aware comparisons.

memory_items.title: Iterative Cell Application with Error Isolation
memory_items.lesson: Apply formulas row-by-row within try/except blocks to handle failures gracefully without halting the entire operation.
memory_items.generalization: Use per-cell exception handling when modifying large ranges to capture partial failures and continue processing unaffected cells.

memory_items.title: Post-Modification Verification Strategy
memory_items.lesson: Sample multiple cells across the modified range to confirm formulas are applied correctly before final save.
memory_items.generalization: Validate cell content at representative positions (beginning, middle, end) after bulk operations to catch formula propagation errors.

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Workbook Integrity First
- **Lesson**: Always create a backup copy of the source file before loading it for modifications.
- **Generalization**: Preserve original file state using copy-before-write pattern; never modify source directly unless explicitly permitted.

### Formula Construction with Reference Scoping
- **Lesson**: When building comparison formulas across groups, use mixed references (absolute range vs relative current-row) to enable proper grouping logic.
- **Generalization**: For conditional aggregation across ranges, construct formulas with absolute range anchors combined with relative current-row references to maintain context-aware comparisons.

### Iterative Cell Application with Error Isolation
- **Lesson**: Apply formulas row-by-row within try/except blocks to handle failures gracefully without halting the entire operation.
- **Generalization**: Use per-cell exception handling when modifying large ranges to capture partial failures and continue processing unaffected cells.

### Post-Modification Verification Strategy
- **Lesson**: Sample multiple cells across the modified range to confirm formulas are applied correctly before final save.
- **Generalization**: Validate cell content at representative positions (beginning, middle, end) after bulk operations to catch formula propagation errors.
